t_ch: 只是，雪原探险这种事，多少还是有点危险……
t_en: But... exploring the Snow Plains is still quite dangerous...

t_ch: 好啦好啦，别这么紧张。她肯定能照顾好自己的。
t_en: Alright, alright, calm down. She'll be able to look after herself.

t_ch: 我的任务，完成了？
t_en: So, mission accomplished?

t_ch: 不管怎么说，这次多亏有{NICKNAME}你在——不然，老弟怕是要担心得觉都睡不好了。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}, for being around for this. Gepard was too anxious to sleep.

t_ch: 我…你太夸张了，姐姐。
t_en: I... Sis, you're exaggerating.

t_ch: 至于之后该怎么和玲宝沟通…我们俩还得再考虑考虑。
t_en: As for how we'll communicate with Lynxy in the future... We'll have to have a good think about it.

t_ch: 你这次真的是帮了我们大忙啊——谢谢你啦，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: You really helped us a lot this time. Thank you so much, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 啊，{NICKNAME}，你也来啦。
t_en: Ah, you're here too.

t_ch: 我来了。
t_en: Yep.

t_ch: 那我们一起等等佩拉吧——她就在整理间里面。
t_en: Then let's wait for Pela together. She's in the storage room.

t_ch: 玲可来得好早。
t_en: You're here early, Lynx.

t_ch: 嗯，只是回去放了行李，顺便冲了个澡。花不了多久。
t_en: Mm-hmm. I went back, stored my supplies, and had a shower. It didn't take long.

t_ch: 我们一起等等佩拉吧——她就在整理间里面。
t_en: Let's wait for Pela together. She's in the storage room.

t_ch: 佩拉还没来吗？
t_en: Is Pela not here yet?

t_ch: 她在整理间里面——我们一起等等她吧。
t_en: She's in the storage room. Let's wait for her.

t_ch: 她在里面干什么？
t_en: What's she doing in there?

t_ch: 我觉得，她应该是在检查那枚软盘的内容…让她一个人静静吧。
t_en: I think she's looking over the contents of that floppy disk... Let's give her some time.

t_ch: 软盘里有什么？
t_en: What's in the floppy disk?

t_ch: 我也不清楚具体内容。但……
t_en: I don't know the exact details, but...

t_ch: 她遇上什么事了吗？
t_en: So, she might be a while...?

t_ch: 我猜，你应该不知道佩拉母亲的事吧？
t_en: I'm guessing you don't know about Pela's mother, right?

t_ch: 佩妮娅•谢尔盖耶夫娜——十六年前意外罹难的、贝洛伯格最优秀的极地探险家。
t_en: Penya Sergeyevna was Belobog's greatest outdoor explorer — passed away sixteen years ago.

t_ch: 这个名字听起来很熟悉。
t_en: That name sounds familiar.

t_ch: 嗯…毕竟是贝洛伯格的名人，你可能在哪也听别人提起过吧。
t_en: She's famous in Belobog. You might have heard other people mention her in passing.

t_ch: 完全没听她提起过。
t_en: Pela never mentioned...

t_ch: 嗯，她一般不会主动对别人说这些。
t_en: Mm, she doesn't usually talk about it.

t_ch: 就这么告诉我了，没问题吗？
t_en: You sure it's okay to tell me this?

t_ch: 我问过了…她说没问题。看来，她真的很信任你。
t_en: I asked her already... and she said it was fine. She seems like she really trusts you.

t_ch: 能看出来，她的性格如此。
t_en: I can understand why...

t_ch: …看来佩拉信任你是有理由的。
t_en: Looks like Pela was right in trusting you.

t_ch: 可可利亚竟然允许外出科考？
t_en: Cocolia allowed explorations outside of the city?

t_ch: 当时的极地探险队隶属于银鬃铁卫，所以是有官方许可的。
t_en: The exploration team belonged to the Silvermane Guards at the time, so they had authorization.

t_ch: …还是言归正传吧。其实，十六年前还发生了一件大事……
t_en: *sigh* Anyway, something else happened sixteen years ago...

t_ch: ——那一年，佩拉出生了。
t_en: ...Pela was born.

t_ch: 你的意思是…
t_en: You mean...
...Yes.

t_ch: 听说那时的佩妮娅女士不顾自己的身孕，坚持驻扎在雪原营地里。虽然不能上一线，但是还可以留在后方做些文书工作。
t_en: They say that despite her pregnancy, Madam Penya insisted on staying at the Snow Plains camp. She couldn't go to the front lines, but she was able to handle administrative duties.

t_ch: 然后，终于有一天…重要的时候到了。
t_en: Then, finally... the day of Pela's birth arrived.

t_ch: 不幸的是，那天发生了严重的意外…再加上探险队遇上了暴风雪，营地的人和外界断了联系…大家当时都束手无策。
t_en: Tragically, there were complications... Other serious incidents occurred that day — the exploration team ran into a blizzard and the camp lost contact with the outside world... No one knew what to do.

t_ch: 唯一值得庆幸的…大概就是佩拉能够顺利诞生吧。
t_en: The only good thing... was that Pela was born healthy.

t_ch: 没想到还有这种故事。
t_en: I never knew any of this...

t_ch: 那软盘又是怎么回事？
t_en: So what about the floppy disk?

t_ch: 软盘——如果科考队的前辈们说的是真的……
t_en: The floppy disk... If the old research team members were telling the truth...

t_ch: …那应该就是佩妮娅女士留下的遗物了。
t_en: ...it was something Madam Penya left behind.

t_ch: 佩妮娅女士生前委托了同事，请他把自己的终端机和软盘带回贝洛伯格。据说，那里面存着她给佩拉留下的「宝物」。
t_en: Madam Penya asked a colleague to take her terminal and floppy disk back to Belobog — she said they contained a "treasure" for Pela.

t_ch: 她说，等到佩拉成年，自然就能够读懂其中的内容了。
t_en: She said that once Pela was older, she'd be able to understand the contents.

t_ch: 然而，那位受佩妮娅女士委托的同事，在返程途中遭到了裂界生物袭击。
t_en: But that colleague was attacked by Fragmentum creatures on his way back to the city.

t_ch: 慌乱中，软盘不知道被落在了哪里…到最后，他只保住了那台终端机。
t_en: During the attack, he dropped the floppy disk in confusion, only managing to bring back the terminal.

t_ch: 至于具体内容，除了佩妮娅女士本人，谁都没有读到过。他们只说她给佩拉留下了好多好多封信——
t_en: Apart from Madam Penya, nobody knew what the floppy disk contained... but they say she left Pela many letters.

t_ch: 从她感知到佩拉时起，一直到分娩那天……
t_en: From the moment she knew she was pregnant, to the day of Pela's birth...

t_ch: …一天都没有落下过。
t_en: ...never missing a day.

t_ch: 妈妈……
t_en: Mom...

t_ch: 妈妈…妈妈……！
t_en: Mom... Mom...!

t_ch: 你还好吗，玲可？
t_en: Lynx, are you okay?

t_ch: 我没事……
t_en: I'm fine...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，陪我去个地方吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, there's somewhere I want to go — will you come too?

t_ch: 好，我跟你走。
t_en: Absolutely.

t_ch: 哪里？
t_en: Where to?

t_ch: …机械屋。
t_en: ...The workshop.

t_ch: 我在那里等你。
t_en: I'll wait for you there.

t_ch: 哥哥，姐姐……
t_en: Serval, Gepard...

t_ch: …我回来了！
t_en: ...I'm back!

t_ch: 玲可！你终于回来了——
t_en: Lynx! You're finally back...

t_ch: 嗯？你的眼睛怎么这么红？发生什么事了？
t_en: Uh, what's with the red eyes? Did something happen?

t_ch: …你怎么还是像块木头一样啊，老弟！
t_en: ...Gepard! Way to make it obvious.

t_ch: 欢迎回家，玲宝。
t_en: Welcome home, Lynxy.
Mm...

t_ch: 乖，没事没事……
t_en: Aww, it's okay. It's okay...

t_ch: 雪原里一定发生了不少事吧——辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Seems like a lot went down in the Snow Plains. Thanks again for your help, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 接下来…让我们稍微独处一会儿吧，谢谢你啦。
t_en: I think we need a little private time. See you soon.

t_ch: （给他们一点私人空间吧……）
t_en: (I should give them some time together...)

t_ch: 对不起…以前是我太任性了……
t_en: I'm sorry... for always being so stubborn...

t_ch: 没关系，我和杰帕德都理解的…对吧，老弟？
t_en: That's okay. Gepard and I understand... right, Gepard?

t_ch: 嗯？啊…对，当然。
t_en: Huh? Ah... Yes, of course.

t_ch: 但是，我还是想说，玲可…我觉得你一个人去城外太危险了……
t_en: But I have to say, Lynx... I think it's too dangerous for you to leave the city on your own...

t_ch: …哎呀，行了行了，你还是别说了！
t_en: *sigh* Gepard! Are you quite finished?

t_ch: （还是不要跑太远吧……）
t_en: (It's probably better not to separate...)

t_ch: （还是留在营地附近比较好……）
t_en: (It's probably best to stick close to the camp...)

t_ch: 小心，玲可——它要发动进攻了！
t_en: Careful, Lynx — It's going to attack!

t_ch: 咱们到了，这里就是铆钉镇…唉，熟悉的景色……
t_en: We're here. This is Rivet Town... *sigh* Everything looks familiar...

t_ch: 走吧，当心点脚下。我很久没回来过了，里头怕是有不少怪物。
t_en: Let's go, and watch your step. I haven't been here in a long time. There are probably some monsters around.

t_ch: 这里什么也看不到…找个高点的地方观望一下吧。
t_en: We can't see anything here... Let's find higher ground to get a better view.

t_ch: 那边，远处山坡上的那幢房子，就是娜塔莎以前的孤儿院。
t_en: Way over there, see that house on the hill? That's Natasha's old orphanage.

t_ch: 奥列格大叔捡到我以后，就把我送到了那里。我童年的一大半时间都是在孤儿院里过的。
t_en: After Oleg found me, he sent me there. I spent over half my childhood in that place.

t_ch: 看着挺气派的…
t_en: It looks quite impressive...

t_ch: 条件不错啊。
t_en: That must be a time of plenty.

t_ch: 我都想在这养老。
t_en: I'd like to retire here.

t_ch: 听娜塔莎说，那座房子是从前某位富商捐赠的。不管他是干什么的，肯定是个大好人。
t_en: Natasha said that the house was donated by some rich businessman. Whatever his business may have been, he must have been a good person.

t_ch: 小时候那段无忧无虑的日子，现在想想还真怀念啊……
t_en: Oh, I miss those carefree days as a kid...

t_ch: 你怎么不说话了？哑巴了吗？
t_en: What? Nothing to say?

t_ch: 不，我只是…觉得有点熟悉，没事。
t_en: No, it's just... this place seems familiar somehow. Never mind.

t_ch: …怪人。
t_en: ...Weirdo.

t_ch: 你们往下看，看到那些杂乱的摊位了吗？那是铆钉镇的集市广场，娜塔莎想要的物资估计就堆放在那儿。
t_en: Look down there. See the mess that remains of those old stalls? That's Rivet Town's market. The supplies Natasha wants are probably there.

t_ch: 小心怪物，继续前进吧。
t_en: Let's keep moving. Watch out for monsters.

t_ch: 小心！前面有敌人。
t_en: Careful! Enemies ahead.

t_ch: 这些家伙可不是流浪者，它们不会——
t_en: These aren't like vagrants. They won't—

t_ch: 你不用把我当新手，我很熟悉裂界里的怪物。准备好了就动手吧！
t_en: Don't treat me like a new recruit. I'm all too familiar with the monsters of the Fragmentum. Let's get ready to fight!

t_ch: 这是…餐厅？
t_en: A... restaurant?

t_ch: 喏，镇上口碑最好的一家。娜塔会带表现好的孩子来改善伙食。
t_en: Heh, the most famous restaurant in town. Natasha would bring the kids who behaved well here for a special meal.

t_ch: 那她带你来过吗？
t_en: So did she ever bring you?

t_ch: 我像那种乖小孩吗？当然没有。
t_en: Do I seem like I was a goody two-shoes? Of course not.

t_ch: 这里的设备好像维护得不错，不像被遗弃很久的样子。
t_en: The facilities here seem to be in pretty good shape, not like those of a place that has been abandoned for years.

t_ch: 被你这么一说，好像确实有点…难道这镇子还有人住？不可能啊……
t_en: Now that you mention it, it does seem a little... Could people still be living here? Impossible...

t_ch: 那边堆了好多货箱，可能有我们想找的东西。
t_en: There's a bunch of crates piled over there. Maybe we can find what we're looking for.

t_ch: 小心周围的怪物。有必要的话，先把这片区域清理干净再说。
t_en: Watch out for monsters. They like to hide. If you need to, clear the area before you dig for the supplies.

t_ch: 该死，怎么全是空的？怪物还会翻箱子吗？
t_en: Darn, how could they all be empty? Now even the monsters are gathering supplies?

t_ch: 裂界生物不需要人类的物资，它们抢东西也不会这么…有礼貌。
t_en: The monsters of the Fragmentum don't need any materials we humans use. And if they did take anything, this place would look much messier...

t_ch: 你的意思是，有人抢先我们一步？
t_en: You mean that someone else got here and took everything before us?

t_ch: 只是猜测——瞧，这边的地上还有踩踏的痕迹。
t_en: It's just a guess, but there are also some footprints on the ground here.

t_ch: 泥土的味道还很新鲜，对方应该没离开太久。
t_en: *sniff* The scent of dirt is still fresh. They can't have come too long before us.

t_ch: 走，就算把这片市集翻个底朝天，也得把东西找出来。
t_en: Let's go then. We need to find something, even if we have to search every corner of this market.

t_ch: …埃里克？
t_en: ...Eric?

t_ch: 喂，小鬼！你怎么一个人跑到这儿来了，不知道这地方有多危险吗？
t_en: Hey, brat! How could you come here by yourself. Don't you know how dangerous this place is?

t_ch: 希、希儿？你、你管得着我吗！我、我想去哪就去哪，跟你们没关系！
t_en: S—Seele? Y—You aren't the boss of me! I—I can go wherever I want, it's none of your beeswax!

t_ch: 你把物资都藏到这儿了？挺能干呀。
t_en: You took the materials and hid them all here? Not bad, kid.

t_ch: 不过，要是我们没解决游荡的怪物，你打算怎么办啊？一直躲在这个角落里发抖吗？
t_en: But if we didn't take care of those monsters, what were you planning to do? Just stay here, shaking in your boots?

t_ch: 还是这么嘴硬，那你倒是说说，你打算怎么当着怪物的面带走几大捆钢板啊？
t_en: Still stubborn, huh? Then tell me, how exactly were you gonna get those big metal plates out of here past those monsters?

t_ch: 谁、谁要你们多管闲事啊？我本来马上就能逃走了，根本用不着大人帮忙！
t_en: Wh—Who asked you to do anything? I was just about to escape, I didn't need your help at all!

t_ch: 别为难他了，他只是个孩子…
t_en: That's enough. He's just a kid...

t_ch: 你别被他唬了，这小鬼是出了名的毛贼。没记错的话，你这衣服也是从其他孩子那儿偷来的吧？
t_en: Don't let him fool you. Everyone in the Underworld knows that this brat is a no-good thief. If I remember correctly, even the clothes you're wearing came off another kid's back?

t_ch: 能分我们些物资吗？救人用。
t_en: Can you share the materials? They're to help injured people.

t_ch: 你不用跟他客气，这小鬼是出了名的毛贼。没记错的话，你这衣服也是从其他孩子那儿偷来的吧？
t_en: Don't be so polite to him. Everyone in the Underworld knows this brat is a no-good thief. If I remember correctly, even the clothes you're wearing came off another kid's back?

t_ch: 那、那是……
t_en: Uh... About that...

t_ch: 说真的，你要钢板跟纱布有什么用？我们可是要拿它们救人的。
t_en: Tell us, what do you even want metal plates and gauze bandages for? We need them to save people.

t_ch: …两百……
t_en: ...<unbreak>200</unbreak>...

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 两百冬城盾……
t_en: Uh... <unbreak>200</unbreak> shield...

t_ch: …我没听错吧？你居然还想谈条件？
t_en: ...Are you kidding me? You want to negotiate?

t_ch: 我身上…一分钱都没有。
t_en: I... don't have any money with me.

t_ch: 我也…我出任务的时候都不带钱包的。
t_en: Me neither... I never bring my coin purse with me on a mission.

t_ch: 狮子大开口啊，这孩子！
t_en: What a rip-off!

t_ch: 两百而已…可惜我出任务的时候没带着钱包。
t_en: It's just <unbreak>200</unbreak>... but I never bring my coin purse with me on a mission.

t_ch: 两百冬城盾是多少钱？
t_en: How much is <unbreak>200</unbreak> shield worth?

t_ch: …不多，但我身上没带着钱包。
t_en: ...Not much, but I didn't bring my coin purse.

t_ch: 你还真打算给他钱啊？都跟你说了，这是他的惯用伎俩——
t_en: You're actually planning to give this little crook money? I'm telling you. This is his same old trick—

t_ch: 才、才不是，这回不一样！我需要钱，因为老爹他……
t_en: No! ...Not this time. I really need money... my daddy...

t_ch: 又把你老爹搬出来当挡箭牌…要是让他知道你总这么干，准没你好果子吃。
t_en: Pretending to use your dad again as an excuse... If he knew about this, you'd be in big trouble.

t_ch: 呜…呜……
希儿！够了，别再说了。
t_en: Seele! That's enough.

t_ch: 埃里克，对吧？这个给你，拿着。
t_en: It's Eric, right? Here, you can have this.

t_ch: 这…这是？
t_en: What... what's this?

t_ch: 这是一枚奖章，筑城者授予我的…看见中间这块蓝色的石头了吗？那是纯度最高的地髓结晶。
t_en: This is a medal given to me by the Architects... See that blue stone in the middle? That's a Geomarrow crystal of the highest purity.

t_ch: 你是因为家人的原因才需要钱，对吧？拿着它，稍微有点见识的买家一眼就能看出它的价值。
t_en: You need money for your family, right? Take this and show it to a knowledgeable buyer, and they'll instantly know how valuable it is.

t_ch: 咻…谢，谢谢姐姐！我……
t_en: *sniffle* ...Th—Thank you! I...

t_ch: …但是你必须答应我一件事——以后再也不准偷别人的东西了，能做到吗？
t_en: But... you'll have to promise me one thing: You'll never steal other people's things again. Promise?

t_ch: 我、我知道了！我以后，绝对不会再偷东西了！
t_en: I—I promise! I'll never steal things again!

t_ch: …那我们就一言为定。如果食言的话，我会亲自带铁卫来抓你喔。奖章收好，赶紧回镇上去吧，路上的障碍我们已经清理干净了。
t_en: ...Then it's a deal. If you go back on your promise, I'm going to bring my Silvermane Guards with me and catch you myself! Here, take the medal and go back to the town. We already took care of any threat on the way. You'll be safe.

t_ch: 谢、谢谢你，不知道名字的姐姐！这些东西，你们都拿去吧！
t_en: Th—Thank you, whoever you are! You guys can take all of these things!

t_ch: 你们，你们也要小心！高处的房子那里，好像有很可怕的东西……
t_en: Y—You guys need to be careful, too! There seems to be something real scary in that house up on the hill...

t_ch: 对、对了！市集正面的路被堵住了…你们要接着往前的话，得从商店街绕路才行。
t_en: Oh, r—right! The road across from the market is blocked... If you wanna go further, you'll have to go to the shopping street.

t_ch: 我们知道了，多谢提醒。
t_en: Got it. Thanks for the tip.

t_ch: 这、这个…总、总会有办法的……
t_en: Um... There's always a way...

t_ch: …那玩意，对你来说挺贵重的吧？
t_en: ...That object was pretty important to you, right?

t_ch: 就这么送给埃里克…没关系吗？
t_en: Are you sure you're okay just giving it to Eric like that?

t_ch: 如果能让他彻底改过，付出这点代价算不了什么。
t_en: If it will make him change his ways, it's more than worth it.

t_ch: 哈，万一他还是没有悔改呢？
t_en: Hah, and what if he doesn't?

t_ch: 从小到大，母亲一直教我宽容，教我存护人们内心的善意…哪怕自身只是一缕微光，也要努力照亮他人。
t_en: From a young age, my mother taught me to be forgiving and to preserve the goodwill in people's hearts... Even if I am but a small glimmer of light in this world, I must try my best to shine brightly for others.

t_ch: 我得给他这个机会，希儿。因为别人给不了他。
t_en: I must give him a chance, Seele, because other people can't.

t_ch: 你说话可真难懂。咱们接着赶路吧。
t_en: I don't really get what you just said. Anyway, ahem, we should get moving.

t_ch: 埃里克说，继续前进的话得从商业街绕路。我还隐约有点印象…往这边走。
t_en: Eric said that we need to take the shopping street to go any further. I think I still remember where it is... this way.

t_ch: 就是这条路，我没记错。顺着它走下去，尽头就是孤儿院了。
t_en: This is the street alright. The orphanage should be at the end.

t_ch: 这些店铺和招牌…总觉得我以前见过。
t_en: These shops and signs... Why do I feel like I've seen them before?

t_ch: 到底是什么时候……
t_en: But when...?

t_ch: 从这里上去就到孤儿院的入口了。
t_en: The entrance to the orphanage is up this way.

t_ch: 不知道那里变成什么样了，说不定还能看到些熟悉的景象。
t_en: I wonder what the old place is like now. Maybe some things will still look familiar.

t_ch: 这些阶梯…感觉变窄了不少。
t_en: These stairs... they seem to have become more narrow.

t_ch: 就快到了！没想到这里保存得还算完整。
t_en: Almost there! I didn't expect this place to be so well preserved.

t_ch: 要是当时大家就能团结起来对抗裂界…说不定我们不用这么快就抛弃家园。
t_en: Y'know, if we could've worked together to push back the Fragmentum... maybe we wouldn't have had to take off and leave our homes behind.

t_ch: 这些箱子已经放在这儿好多年了吧？确定里面还有能用的物资吗……
t_en: These crates have been sitting here for many years, right? Even if they have materials in them, are you sure they are still usable...?

t_ch: 你该不会以为下层区的人还会在乎保质期吧？有得用就不错了。
t_en: Do you think undergrounders care about expiration dates? Even having any supplies is a miracle in itself.

t_ch: 没有…没有…空的……
t_en: No... No... Empty...

t_ch: 怎么全是空的，到底是谁——
t_en: How are they all empty? Who could've—

t_ch: ——小心！背后有敌人！
t_en: Careful! Enemy behind you!

t_ch: 这么大的家伙…
t_en: That's a big one...

t_ch: 看上去不太好对付…
t_en: It looks tough...

t_ch: 我只在禁区前线见过这种怪物……
t_en: I've only seen this kind of monster before in the restricted zone...

t_ch: 哼，管你是谁——居然敢从背后偷袭，我可不会放过你！
t_en: Hmph, I don't care how big you are. You dare sneak up on us from behind. This is it for you, pal!

t_ch: 哈…不过如此嘛。
t_en: Ha... That was it?

t_ch: 我们可是正义的三打一。
t_en: Well, it was three against one.

t_ch: 哼，我一个人也应付得来啊。不过…跟你们一块战斗确实很痛快就是了。
t_en: Nah, I could've taken it on by myself. But still... fighting alongside you two was pretty fun.

t_ch: 你一个人的话可就难了。
t_en: It wouldn't have been so easy on your own though.

t_ch: 哼，你瞧不起我？不过…跟你们一块战斗确实很痛快就是了。
t_en: Please, you don't think I could do it? But still... fighting alongside you two was pretty fun.

t_ch: 这下周围应该就清净了，我们接着到里面找找吧。
t_en: Should be all clear now. Let's poke around.

t_ch: 那边的角落里，我依稀记得有些存货。我们小时候经常在户外打闹，皮外伤是常有的事。
t_en: The kids at the orphanage were always getting into scraps when we played out here. If I remember it right, there ought to be some supplies for our cuts and bruises stored in that corner there.

t_ch: 但愿物资没被刚才那家伙毁掉。
t_en: I just hope the monster didn't get to them.

t_ch: …不是幻觉。
t_en: ...It wasn't my imagination.

t_ch: 啊？你在说啥？
t_en: Huh? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 这幢建筑，还有这些小孩子的游艺设施…我见过。
t_en: This building, this playground equipment... I've seen it all before.

t_ch: 哈？少来，上下层封锁十来年，一个银鬃铁卫也没下来过，你这样的大小姐光临地底，那绝对是个大新闻。
t_en: What!? Come on, the overground and underground have been sealed off from each other for over ten years. It would've been news if a Silvermane Guard had come down here, let alone an Overworld princess.

t_ch: ——等会儿，难道，在那之前……
t_en: Wait, unless, before that...

t_ch: …嗯，有这个可能性。也只有这样才能解释，为什么我的记忆这么模糊。
t_en: ...Yes, it's a possibility. That would explain why my memory of it is so blurry.

t_ch: 走吧，我得再多看看周围才能确定……
t_en: Come on, I need to look around a bit more before I can be sure...

t_ch: 只剩这里没找了…但愿我没记错。
t_en: We've looked everywhere but here... I hope my memory is right.

t_ch: 这是——啊，找到了！
t_en: It's... Ah! Yes, we found it!

t_ch: 太棒了，几乎完好无损！娜塔估计也想不到吧，她几年前存放的物资居然保存得这么好。
t_en: Great, and it's barely damaged! Nat wouldn't have expected her old supplies from years ago to have stayed in such good condition.

t_ch: 酒精这种东西还是检查下保质期吧，如果过期了就没什么效用了。
t_en: You should still check the expiration date for things like rubbing alcohol. If it's expired, it won't be effective.

t_ch: 说的也是，那我拿几瓶出来看看。
t_en: Hm, true. Let me check a few bottles.

t_ch: …咦？这是什么？
t_en: ...Huh? What's this?

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: This is...

t_ch: 这是…我小时候的东西。
t_en: This is... something from when I was a child.

t_ch: 你、你小时候？！你确定吗……
t_en: Wh—When you were a child!? Are you sure...?

t_ch: 你小时候来过这镇子？
t_en: You came to this town as a child?

t_ch: 不是的，我现在记起来了……
t_en: No, I remember now...

t_ch: 同款而已吧。
t_en: Just a coincidence. Lots of kids probably have this toy.

t_ch: 这世界上没有那么巧的事。我现在记起来了……
t_en: There is no such coincidence in this world. I remember now...

t_ch: 难道你小时候……
t_en: Could it be, when you were a child...

t_ch: 嗯，我现在记起来了……
t_en: Yes. I remember now...

t_ch: 我以前住在这里——就在筑城者将我带走，可可利亚大人收养我之前——我住在这里！我…是下层区的人。
t_en: I—I used to live here... B—Before the Architects took me away, before Madam Cocolia adopted me... I used to live here! I'm... an undergrounder.

t_ch: 你是…下层区人？不对，你说可可利亚——你是那个「大守护者」的养女？那——
t_en: You're... an undergrounder? Wait a minute... you're saying that Cocolia, the Supreme Guardian, adopted you? Then—

t_ch: 是的，我是筑城者指定的继承人。将来，我会成为领导贝洛伯格的「守护者」。
t_en: Yes. I'm the successor chosen by the Architects. In the future, I will become the guardian who leads Belobog.

t_ch: 为什么，为什么我到现在才想起来？小时候的记忆好模糊……
t_en: Why... Why didn't I remember this until now? My childhood memories are so blurry...

t_ch: 你当时年龄太小了。
t_en: You were too little to remember clearly.

t_ch: 可可利亚做了手脚。
t_en: Cocolia must have done something to make you forget.

t_ch: 不，母亲不会那么对我的…绝对不会。
t_en: No, mother would never do that to me... there's no way she would.

t_ch: 筑城者们说过，对「守护者」继承人的挑选会在整个贝洛伯格范围内进行，过程短则几年，长则数十年。
t_en: The Architects said that the guardian's successor is selected from among all people in Belobog. The selection process can take anywhere from a few years to a few decades.

t_ch: 这段时间里，上下层区每个到了年纪的孩子都会接受测试，最终只有一个拥有「资格」的孩子会被选中。
t_en: During that time, all children of the appropriate age in both the Overworld and the Underworld will be tested. In the end, only one child deemed as "worthy" will be chosen.

t_ch: 我好像…就是在这里被选中，然后被带上地面的……
t_en: I... must have been chosen from among the children here and then taken to the surface...

t_ch: 孤儿院的孩子总是来来去去…真没想到，我们中居然出了个未来的大守护者！娜塔知道这事吗？难道她一直瞒着……
t_en: The kids at the orphanage are always coming and going... I never thought that there would've been a future Supreme Guardian among us! Does Nat know about this? Could she have been keeping it a secret...

t_ch: …我不觉得她知道。守护者继承人的选拔是绝对的机密，被选中的孩子必须完全告别过去。
t_en: ...I don't think she knows. The selection of the successor is done with utmost secrecy, and the child chosen must completely say goodbye to their past.

t_ch: 所以我才会成为可可利亚大人的女儿——除了下一任守护者，我没有别的身份。但我，我差得太远了……
t_en: And thus... I became the daughter of Madam Cocolia. My only identity is that of guardian successor, but I'm... not good enough...

t_ch: 我每天都目睹铁卫战士在裂界里牺牲，却始终下不定决心改变母亲大人的想法。
t_en: Every day I see Silvermane Guards sacrifice their lives in the Fragmentum, but I've never had the resolve to change mother's mind.

t_ch: 我明明知道下层区在受苦，却什么忙都帮不上。曾经的家园变成了这副模样，我却还完全被蒙在鼓里……
t_en: The Underworld has been struggling, but I never tried to help. I didn't even know that it was my own home that had fallen into such a dire state...

t_ch: 到头来，我什么都「守护」不了。这样的我为什么会被选中，我怎么能胜任……
t_en: In the end, I haven't been able to guard anything. How could they have thought I was worthy to be the successor...

t_ch: 喂——你说够了没有啊？
t_en: Hey! Are you done?
...Huh?

t_ch: 哭哭唧唧的，吵死了。
t_en: Wah wah wah... So annoying.
I... What?

t_ch: 干嘛？自我感动几句，以为我会同情你吗？你可以躲在宫殿里胡思乱想，上前线也不用拿自己的命去赌，可下层区的人呢？有上顿没下顿，能活着就算不错了！
t_en: What? You thought I should feel sorry for you after your little speech? You can hide in your palace while you fuss over your Overworld problems, and on the front line it's not even you who's putting their life in danger. But in the Underworld, what do we worry about? If we'll have food for our next meal or survive to the next week!

t_ch: 「哪怕自身只是半缕微光，也要照亮他人」——这是你自己说的吧。你不是想保护所有人吗？比起在这儿哭哭啼啼，你就没有更要紧的事做了吗？！
t_en: Even if I am but a small glimmer of light in this world, I must try my best to shine brightly for others. Didn't you say that? Well, if you really want to protect everyone, what are you standing here crying for? Get to it!

t_ch: 的确，自怜自艾解决不了任何问题。
t_en: You're right... You're right, self-pity doesn't help solve anything.

t_ch: 谢谢你，希儿。通常我陷进负面思绪的时候，旁边总是安抚的声音；从来没有一个人敢像你这样，直接把我敲醒。
t_en: Thank you, Seele. Usually when I feel sorry for myself, I am met with gentle consolation. Never has anyone dared to be tough and make me snap out of it.

t_ch: 哼，安抚我不会，打醒你要几次都行。
t_en: Hmph. I'm no good at consoling, but I can hit you on the head a few times. No problem.

t_ch: …我一看就知道，你心里的包袱太重了。干嘛把自己逼到那种地步？
t_en: ...I can tell that you have a big burden to carry. Why do you force yourself to?

t_ch: …身为贝洛伯格未来的守护者，我必须随时审视自己的行为和思想。
t_en: ...As the successor to the guardian of Belobog, I must always be vigilant of my behavior and thoughts.

t_ch: 是是是，大小姐～哼，未来的大守护者，居然和我出身同一个孤儿院…真是孽缘。
t_en: Yeah yeah, Lady Bronya~ Hmph, Miss Future Supreme Guardian comes from the same orphanage as me... Who thought it would be a good idea to put us together, huh?

t_ch: 要不要我带你在孤儿院周围转转？跟你不一样，小时候的事我可都记得一清二楚。
t_en: How about I show you around the orphanage a bit more? Unlike you, my memories from when I was little are crystal clear.

t_ch: 嗯，那我就稍微陪你走神一小会儿吧。
t_en: Okay, I'll indulge in old memories with you for a while.

t_ch: （好像我来得不是时候，还是让那两个人独处一会儿吧……）
t_en: (I should let those two be alone for a bit...)

t_ch: （要找的只剩下止痛剂了——在附近找找吧。）
t_en: (The only thing left to find is painkillers. I'll see if there's any nearby.)

t_ch: 嘿…嘿咻……
（这声音是……）
t_en: (What's that sound...)

t_ch: 找到啦，就在这里！谢谢你，帕金斯。
t_en: Oh! It's here, we found it! Thank you, Perkins!

t_ch: 哔——不用谢。克拉拉。
t_en: Beep — Don't mention it, Clara.

t_ch: 警告。警告。发现威胁——
t_en: Warning. Warning. Danger detected—

t_ch: 我没有恶意——
t_en: I mean no harm—

t_ch: 我只是路过——
t_en: I'm just passing by—

t_ch: 我命休矣——
t_en: I'm a goner—

t_ch: 保护克拉拉。开启主动防卫模式——
t_en: Protect Clara. Initiating active defense mode—

t_ch: 帕金斯，不要！快停下！
t_en: Perkins, don't! Stop!

t_ch: 我见过这个人，{F#她}{M#他}…{F#她}{M#他}不是坏人，对吧？
t_en: I've seen this person before. {F#Sh—She's}{M#H—He's} not bad, right?

t_ch: 收到指令。威胁消除。主动防卫模式关闭。
t_en: Command received. Danger removed. Deactivating active defense mode.

t_ch: 这个地方很危险，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}快回去吧。
t_en: This place is dangerous. You should leave.

t_ch: 我是来给伤员找药的。
t_en: I'm looking for medicine to help the injured.

t_ch: 啊，克拉拉也是为了这些止痛剂来的。
t_en: Oh? I came here for these painkillers, too.

t_ch: 流浪者营地有很多受伤的人，矿队也一样吧？要是大家能和平共处就好了……
t_en: There are a lot of people hurt at the vagrant camp, I guess the miners are no different? *sigh* If everyone could just get along, things would be easier...

t_ch: 你快回去才是。
t_en: Shouldn't you be the one leaving, missy?

t_ch: 克拉拉有帕金斯陪着，不会有事的。我收拾好这批止痛剂就会走啦。
t_en: Perkins is protecting me. I'll be alright. I'm going to leave after I gather up these painkillers.

t_ch: …啊，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}也是为了这些物资而来的吗？
t_en: ...Uh, is that what you're here for too?

t_ch: 也对，矿队那边肯定也有不少受伤的人吧？要是大家都能和平共处就好了……
t_en: That would make sense. I'm sure a lot of miners are injured, right? *sigh* If everyone could just get along, things would be easier...

t_ch: 给，这些药给你，希望有用……
t_en: Mm... Here you are. I hope the medicine is of help to you...

t_ch: 你和史瓦罗是什么关系？
t_en: What's your relationship with Svarog?

t_ch: 史瓦罗也在这里吗？
t_en: Is Svarog here, too?

t_ch: 不在。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生他…他是克拉拉的家人。我很小的时候就遇到了史瓦罗先生，是他把我带在身边养大，让我成为了他的家人。
t_en: Mr. Svarog, he... he's my family. I met Mr. Svarog when I was real little. He took me in and took care of me.

t_ch: 之前在大矿区的事，真是对不起…史瓦罗先生不信任除了克拉拉以外的人类，尤其是「地火」的各位。
t_en: I'm really sorry about what happened back in the Great Mine... Mr. Svarog doesn't trust any humans besides me, especially everyone from Wildfire.

t_ch: 史瓦罗为什么要攻击我们？
t_en: Why did Svarog attack us?

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生没有伤害大家的意思，他只是相信自己的计算结果…他觉得和人们交涉的效率总是很低。
t_en: Mr. Svarog doesn't want to hurt people, but he always follows the calculations of his analysis. He thinks that interaction with humans is... inefficient.

t_ch: 史瓦罗为什么要介入矿区事件？
t_en: Why did Svarog want to get involved in the incident in the mines?

t_ch: 因为他听说了有矿工和流浪者们发生了大规模的争斗…他的本意是想阻止大家为了矿脉流血。
t_en: He heard that the miners and vagrants had begun to engage in a large-scale conflict... He wanted to stop everyone from getting hurt fighting over the mines.

t_ch: 「地火」的大家，想要带人们离开下层…但是史瓦罗先生希望大家留在这里。因为在他的计算里，地上已经不再安全了。
t_en: Wildfire wants everyone to leave the underground... But Mr. Svarog wants everyone to stay here. According to his calculations, the overground is already unsafe.

t_ch: 难道下层区就安全吗？
t_en: But the underground is safer?

t_ch: 至少在史瓦罗先生的计算里，是这样没错……
t_en: Yes, at least according to Mr. Svarog's calculations...

t_ch: 他的系统出故障了。
t_en: There must be some error in his system.

t_ch: 不，不是那样的…史瓦罗先生很健康，他的系统一直都有在维护。
t_en: No, there can't be... Mr. Svarog is healthy, his system is always being maintained.

t_ch: 所以他才守着大柱子，不让人离开？
t_en: So he stands guard at the huge pillar stopping people from leaving?

t_ch: 嗯…史瓦罗先生很执着。
t_en: Yes... Mr. Svarog is very stubborn.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生说他的职责是「存护」。为此，他必须作出能够保存人类的决定。
t_en: He says that his responsibility is "preservation." To do that, he must make decisions that best protect humanity.

t_ch: 裂界迟早会把地底全部吞掉。
t_en: The Fragmentum will swallow up all of the underground sooner or later.

t_ch: 他怎么和大守护者一个德性。
t_en: Sounds a lot like the Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 我知道「地火」的大家都很想和史瓦罗先生谈谈…但不管来多少次，史瓦罗先生的态度都不会转变的……
t_en: I know that Wildfire wants to talk to Mr. Svarog... But no matter how many times you talk to him, Mr. Svarog won't change his mind...

t_ch: 所以，请相信克拉拉！克拉拉会尽力去说服史瓦罗先生。迟早有一天，他能听进去我的话……
t_en: So, please trust me! I'll try to persuade Mr. Svarog. Hopefully, one day he will finally listen...

t_ch: …克拉拉？你怎么会在这里？
t_en: ...Clara? What are you doing here?

t_ch: 你是…「地火」的希儿姐姐？我来这里是为了……
t_en: You're... Miss Seele from Wildfire? I came here to...

t_ch: 你和克拉拉将刚才交谈的内容转告给了希儿和布洛妮娅。
t_en: You and Clara tell Seele and Bronya what you just discussed...

t_ch: …你为什么要帮流浪者找药？他们不是本事很大吗，怎么好意思让一个小姑娘跑东跑西？
t_en: ...Why are you helping the vagrants look for medicine? If they're so great, how could they make a little girl go around running errands for them?

t_ch: 不、不是这样的，克拉拉没有受人委托…只是，流浪者们的生存条件很差，他们的聚落里也没有诊所，所以克拉拉才想来帮帮他们。
t_en: N—No, it's not like that. I wasn't made to do anything... It's just that, the living conditions for the vagrants aren't great. There's not even a clinic in their camp. So, I wanted to help them out.

t_ch: 而且，其实他们不都是坏人…大家都只想好好地活下去。
t_en: Also, not all of them are bad people... everyone just wants to survive.

t_ch: 希儿姐姐，这批药原来是娜塔莎姐姐的吗？那…可以给我一些吗？流浪者们也伤得很重。
t_en: Miss Seele, did this medicine belong to Miss Natasha? Then... can you give me some? The vagrants' injuries are quite serious.

t_ch: 我知道了，那这些止痛剂，咱们就平分了吧。
t_en: *sigh* I see. Let's split these painkillers then.

t_ch: 真、真的可以吗？不用问娜塔莎姐姐的意思？
t_en: R—Really? We don't need to ask Miss Natasha first?

t_ch: 我了解娜塔，她不会介意的。虽说这样会让我俩的善后工作翻倍…不过她肯定能理解，一向都是这么过来的。
t_en: If I know Nat, she won't mind. Although this will make it more difficult for us... she'll understand. She always does.

t_ch: 那真是太好了，谢谢希儿姐姐……
t_en: Great, thank you, Miss Seele...

t_ch: 要走了吗？这旧镇太危险了，我们送你一段吧。
t_en: Are you leaving? This old town is too dangerous. We'll accompany you part of the way.

t_ch: 没、没关系的，克拉拉还有帕金斯陪，而且…我在这里还有些东西要找。
t_en: I—It's fine, I have Perkins for company. And... I have some more things I need to look for here first.

t_ch: 我知道了，那你一定要小心。
t_en: Understood. Be careful, though.

t_ch: 钢板和纱布…医用酒精…止痛剂。好，这样就齐活了。
t_en: Metal plates and bandages... rubbing alcohol... painkillers. Great, that's everything.

t_ch: 快走吧，我预估的时间应该还赶得上。回磐岩镇找娜塔莎交差吧。
t_en: Let's go report to Natasha in Boulder Town, it won't take long to get back.

t_ch: 我们到了！居然已经隔了这么久了啊…这里倒是一点都没变。
t_en: ...We made it! It's been so long... but this place hasn't changed one bit.

t_ch: 前面好像堆了很多杂物，过去看看有没有娜塔需要的酒精吧。
t_en: Oh! It looks like there's a lot of stuff piled outside. Let's go see if there's any rubbing alcohol for Nat.

t_ch: 喂，你别到处乱跑！先在孤儿院这边找找吧。
t_en: Hey, where are you going!? Let's look around the orphanage first.

t_ch: 我找到了这个，你拿着吧。
t_en: I found this. Here, take it.

t_ch: 这是什么…胸针？
t_en: What is this? ...A brooch?

t_ch: 娜塔莎经常教我们做手工，这应该就是某个孩子的杰作了。
t_en: Natasha would often teach us how to make different handicrafts. This must be some kid's masterpiece.

t_ch: 为什么要给我？
t_en: And why give it to me?

t_ch: 你不是把那什么奖章送给埃里克了吗？这玩意没那么值钱，但好歹算个替代。
t_en: You gave your medal to that kid Eric. This thing might not be as valuable, but it's at least a replacement of sorts.

t_ch: 但这是别人辛辛苦苦做出来的吧？这样随便拿走的话……
t_en: But didn't someone work hard to make this? I can't just take it from here...

t_ch: …你可真死板，叫你收下就收下呗。
t_en: ...You sure are prissy, huh? Just take it.

t_ch: 这上面是什么，小孩子的玩具吗？
t_en: What's up here? Some kind of toy?

t_ch: 是啊。你选一个吧，红的还是蓝的？
t_en: Yep. You pick, red or blue?

t_ch: 嗯…蓝色的吧。
t_en: Hm... blue.

t_ch: 哈！你看，这都十几年了，这上面的牙印居然还在。
t_en: Ha! Look at this. After over ten years, the teeth marks are still here.

t_ch: 牙印？
t_en: Teeth marks?

t_ch: 我记起来了！当时有个大孩子想跟我抢玩具，我拗不过他，所以干脆就把它紧紧咬住……
t_en: Alright, well... this one time, a big kid was trying to steal this toy from me. I couldn't outwrestle him for it, so I just bit down hard and hung on for dear life.

t_ch: …真粗野。
t_en: ...How boorish.

t_ch: 这秋千上全是灰…喂，别坐，太脏了！
t_en: This swing is covered in dust... Hey, don't sit on it! It's filthy!

t_ch: 真让人怀念啊…还记得我有一次荡得太高，把旁边所有人都吓出一身汗。
t_en: Ah, it sure brings back memories... I still remember one time I swung super high, and everyone was scared I would fly off.

t_ch: …荡个秋千也能这么刺激吗？
t_en: ...Who knew swinging could be so intense?

t_ch: 我当时一门心思想飞上天去，把自己荡到上层区看看。
t_en: At the time, I wanted to swing myself into the sky and fly up to the Overworld.

t_ch: 太危险了…你能活到现在真是个奇迹。
t_en: So dangerous... You're lucky to have lived till now.

t_ch: 哼，谁说不是呢？
t_en: Hmph, aren't we all, though?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你怎么才到？虎克都在这里等了好久了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what took you so long? Hook's been waiting here for ages!

t_ch: 让你久等了。
t_en: Sorry to have kept you waiting.

t_ch: …其实也没有等那么久啦，你人到了就行！
t_en: ...Well, it wasn't actually that long. As long as you're here now, it's fine!

t_ch: 我可是一刻不停地赶过来的。
t_en: But I didn't take a single break on my way over.

t_ch: …你说是就是吧。不过没关系，人到了就行！
t_en: ...Okay, whatever you say. As long as you're here now, it's fine!

t_ch: 虎克有一个好消息，还有一个坏消息——你想先听哪个？
t_en: I got some good news and some bad news. Which do you wanna hear first?

t_ch: 先说坏消息吧。
t_en: The bad news.

t_ch: 虎克把计划告诉了鼹鼠党的其他小伙伴，结果尤利安让我也帮他带个宝贝回去。要是不帮他的话，他就要把咱们的计划抖给老巫婆！
t_en: I told the rest of The Moles about the plan, and Julian asked me to get some treasure for him. If we don't help him, he'll tell the old witch where we've gone!

t_ch: 好消息是，他愿意资助我们的冒险。你看，这把花园铲就是他给我的！
t_en: The good news is that he's willing to contribute to our adventure. Look, he gave me this gardening shovel!

t_ch: 先说好消息吧。
t_en: The good news.

t_ch: 尤利安打算资助我们的冒险，你看，这把花园铲就是他给我的！
t_en: Julian is willing to contribute to our adventure. Look, he gave me this gardening shovel!

t_ch: 坏消息嘛，就是他要我们也帮他找一件宝贝…否则他就去找老巫婆告状！
t_en: The bad news is that he asked us to help him get some treasure... Otherwise, he'll tell the old witch about our plan!

t_ch: 放肆，他敢威胁鼹鼠党的首领？
t_en: How dare he threaten the boss of The Moles!

t_ch: 算了算了，反正尤利安也帮过虎克不少忙！这次就当还他的人情吧。
t_en: Well, Julian has been a huge help to me! Consider this returning the favor.

t_ch: 你就这么怕娜塔莎？
t_en: Are you really that scared of Natasha?

t_ch: 胡、胡说什么！漆黑的虎克大人谁都不怕！
t_en: Hehehehe her...? No, what? N—Nonsense, no! Nobody scares Pitch-Dark Hook the Great!

t_ch: 宝贝可不会长出腿自己来找我们！快点，咱们出发吧！
t_en: Well, the treasure won't grow legs and find us, will it! Come on, let's go!

t_ch: 尤利安的老爹老妈以前就是在这附近做生意的。我们离开镇子的时候，他家有很多东西都没来得及带走。
t_en: Julian's parents used to own a business in this area. When we were all forced to leave the town, there were lots of things they didn't have time to take with them.

t_ch: 他的宝贝应该不会太难找吧！那边，我们去翻翻看！
t_en: ...It shouldn't be too hard to locate his treasure! Let's start by looking over there!

t_ch: 啊，找到尤利安要的宝贝了！不是那串钥匙，是这些玻璃珠。你看，这有好多种颜色呀！想集齐这一整套可不容易嗷。
t_en: Ah, we found Julian's treasure! No, not the key — these glass marbles. Look at all the different colors! I bet it wasn't easy collecting all these.

t_ch: 这些珠子有什么用？
t_en: What are these marbles used for?

t_ch: 啊？你怎么连这都不知道，难道你没玩过打弹珠游戏嘛？
t_en: What? Hee-hee, you don't know? Have you never played marbles before!?

t_ch: 话说回来，这把钥匙是拿来开什么的呀？说不定尤利安知道点啥……
t_en: By the way, what does this key open? Maybe Julian knows...

t_ch: 我以为钥匙才是宝物…
t_en: I thought the key was the treasure...

t_ch: 不是哦，这些弹珠才是他要找的东西。
t_en: No, these marbles are what he's after.

t_ch: 不过虎克也好好奇呀…这把钥匙是拿来开什么的呢？回去以后再问问尤利安吧。
t_en: But I'm also curious... What does this key open? Let's ask Julian when we get back.

t_ch: 你那是什么表情呀！虎克知道了，你觉得这罐玻璃珠根本不值钱，对不对？
t_en: What's that look on your face? Oh, I get it. You don't think this jar of glass marbles is worth anything, right?

t_ch: 感觉没什么特别的。
t_en: It doesn't look like anything special.

t_ch: 我可没这么想…
t_en: That's not what I was thinking...

t_ch: 哼，别装啦！你的表情已经把你出卖啦！
t_en: Hmph, don't even try to pretend otherwise! The look on your face sold you out!

t_ch: 告诉你吧，这罐玻璃珠对鼹鼠党来说，可是有非常重大的意义哦。
t_en: Let me tell you something, this jar of marbles means a lot to The Moles.

t_ch: 就是靠着它，尤利安帮鼹鼠党解决了超超超大危机，坐到了二把手的位置！
t_en: Julian used it to help The Moles overcome a super-huge crisis, for which he was promoted to second-in-command!

t_ch: 他用弹珠打败了裂界怪物?
t_en: He defeated Fragmentum monsters with marbles?

t_ch: 当然不是，但功绩也不次于那个哦！
t_en: Of course it wasn't that. But his accomplishments are nothing to be scoffed at!

t_ch: 你说的大危机…
t_en: The crisis you're referring to...

t_ch: 是威胁到鼹鼠党尊严的大危机哦！
t_en: It was a super-huge crisis that jeopardized The Moles' honor!

t_ch: 从前，孤儿院的小孩子跟镇上的小孩子可是死对头！那些家伙仗着有爸妈给的零花钱，经常来欺负我们。
t_en: In the past, the kids in town were archrivals to us at the orphanage! Those kids used to bully us, relying on the pocket money that their parents gave 'em.

t_ch: 有一次，他们给鼹鼠党发来了挑战书，说是要和我们进行打弹珠比赛！
t_en: One time, they sent us a formal declaration of war — challenging The Moles to a marbles competition!

t_ch: 其实大家都知道，我们的实力相差有多大！孤儿院的大家都只有在路边捡到的玻璃珠，根本比不上那些有钱孩子的弹珠！
t_en: We were all well aware that we didn't stand a chance! We at the orphanage only had some old glass marbles we scavenged from the side of the road, which were no match for those rich kids' marbles!

t_ch: 不过就算这样，尤利安还是决定勇敢应战——而且还赢了一大堆五颜六色的玻璃珠回来，真是太神气啦！
t_en: Nonetheless, Julian bravely accepted the challenge... and triumphantly returned with a pile of colorful glass marbles he won! It was incredible!

t_ch: 那他还真是个小英雄。
t_en: Wow, what a little hero.

t_ch: 难怪这些弹珠对他那么重要。
t_en: It's no wonder he values these marbles so much.

t_ch: 可不是嘛，这下你懂了吧，为什么他要拜托我们来找这件宝物。
t_en: Yep. So now you know why he wanted us to find this treasure.

t_ch: 好啦，闲话就聊到这里吧！接下来该去找虎克的宝贝了。
t_en: Alright, enough chit-chat! Next we need to find my treasure.

t_ch: 反正就是小孩子过家家呗？
t_en: Sounds like kids playing make-believe!

t_ch: 大人高高在上的态度又来了，这次是真正的大危机！
t_en: You're being a rude adult again... Believe me, I'm not exaggerating when I say that crisis was super huge!

t_ch: 就只是这样？
t_en: That's it?

t_ch: 虎克给你说了这么多，你就这么点儿感想吗？还不如说给盐烧洞螈听呢。
t_en: After that epic story, that's all you have to say? Ugh... I would've been better off telling a stone-grilled olm instead.

t_ch: 该做正事啦！虎克的宝贝就藏在孤儿院下边，矿区入口附近。
t_en: Time to get to work! Hook's treasure is hidden below the orphanage, near the mine's entrance.

t_ch: 总感觉…这里跟虎克印象中的铆钉镇很不一样。不会连孤儿院也变成这样了吧？
t_en: This doesn't feel like the Rivet Town I remember... Do you think the orphanage is like this now too?

t_ch: 这片地方，跟虎克离开的时候完全不一样了！箱子和矿车都被摆在大街上，好乱呀。虎克的宝贝该不会也被人搬走了吧？
t_en: This place is nothing like it was before I left! Boxes and mine carts are all over the street. What a mess! Did someone move my treasure too?

t_ch: 据说堆在这里是为了挡住怪物。
t_en: Perhaps these are stacked here to keep the monsters away.

t_ch: 谁知道呢？
t_en: Who knows?

t_ch: 虎克以前把它放在矿队的杂物堆那边。那个墙角后面，堆了好多地髓，还有好多好多的箱子。应该不会被挪走吧？
t_en: I used to keep it in the mining team's junk pile. Behind the corner, there were heaps of Geomarrow and boxes. Hopefully, it hasn't been taken.

t_ch: 这就是所有线索了吗？
t_en: Is that all the info you have?

t_ch: 虎克就记得这么多。不过你放心，我也会跟你一起找的！说不定还能比你先找到呢。
t_en: That's all I can recall. But don't worry, I'll look with you! Heh, I may even find it before you.

t_ch: 明白了，我去找找吧。
t_en: Got it, I'll start looking.

t_ch: 虎克也一起来找，肯定能找到的。
t_en: I'll look with you. We'll definitely find it if we work together.

t_ch: 真没用呀，荣誉队员！到最后还是得靠漆黑的虎克大人亲自出手嘛。
t_en: What a shame, honorary member! In the end, we still had to rely on Pitch-Dark Hook the Great herself.

t_ch: 快，快来帮帮我！东西太高了，我够不着！
t_en: Quick, help! I can't reach it, it's too high!

t_ch: 你看，就在这个盒子里！这台小钻机是老爹矿队里的叔叔送给我的，也是虎克的生日礼物哦！
t_en: Look, it's in this box! This miniature drill rig was given to me by a guy from daddy's mining team as a birthday present!

t_ch: 虎克现在就把它打开——嘿咻！
t_en: I'm going to turn it on... Whoosh!
Huh...?

t_ch: 坏了？
t_en: It's broken?
Ohh...

t_ch: 还好用吗？
t_en: Is it working?

t_ch: 怎…怎么启动不了……
t_en: It... it won't turn on...

t_ch: 先、先不要跟虎克说话…让虎克冷静一下！
t_en: J—Just don't talk to me right now... I need to calm down!

t_ch: 在这里干等着也不是办法。
t_en: Standing around waiting won't do anything.

t_ch: 不是都说了嘛，先不要和我说话！虎克必须好好想想……
t_en: I said don't talk to me right now! I need time to think...

t_ch: （适当的沉默）
t_en: (Brief silence)

t_ch: （继续适当的沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent a bit longer)

t_ch: （沉默真的有用吗？）
t_en: (Does silence actually help?)

t_ch: 荣誉队员…我只是好奇，你觉得尤利安的玻璃珠能卖多少钱？
t_en: Honorary member... Um, I wonder, how much do you think Julian's glass marbles would sell for?

t_ch: 这就是你冷静的结果？！
t_en: That's all you came up with after calming down!?

t_ch: 你还当真了吗？虎克其实根本没有这个打算，再说了，玻璃珠子根本值不了钱。
t_en: ...Uhh, what? Hehehe, did you think I was serious? I wasn't actually planning on selling these marbles — besides, they aren't even worth much.

t_ch: 那是尤利安的东西…
t_en: But these marbles belong to Julian...

t_ch: 虎克其实根本没有这个打算…不过，尤利安拿到了宝贝，虎克却什么都没有…好亏！
t_en: ...*sigh* I wasn't actually planning on selling these marbles... But Julian gets his treasure and I get nothing... What a lousy bargain!

t_ch: 去找人修修如何？
t_en: Why not find someone to fix it?

t_ch: 那倒是可以…但是修理，也是要花不少钱的吧？还是好亏！
t_en: That might work... but it'll be pretty expensive to fix, right?

t_ch: 找那个叔叔帮你如何？
t_en: How about we ask the guy who gave you the gift for help?

t_ch: 不行，老爹说那个叔叔走丢了，后来再也没见过他。听说，他好像是去了很遥远的地方……
t_en: Not possible. Daddy said that guy went missing one day, and he never saw him again. Maybe he went to someplace far away...

t_ch: 虎克也不知道他什么时候会回来，肯定不行啦！
t_en: I have no idea when he'll come back. So that definitely won't work!

t_ch: 小钻机很珍贵吗？
t_en: Is the drill rig really that valuable?

t_ch: 跟洞洞机肯定没得比啦。但那位叔叔的手很巧，总能做出些很精妙的小玩意。
t_en: Not as much as Diggertron, but that guy who gave it to me was pretty skillful. He could always make fancy things.

t_ch: 让那个叔叔帮你修修吧。
t_en: How about we ask him to help you fix it?

t_ch: 不行，老爹说那个叔叔走丢了，后来再也没见过他。听说，他好像是去了很遥远的地方……
t_en: Not possible. Daddy said that guy went missing one day, and he never saw him again. Maybe he went to someplace far away...

t_ch: 虎克也不知道他什么时候会回来，肯定不行啦！
t_en: I have no idea when he'll come back. So that definitely won't work!

t_ch: 现在怎么办？
t_en: What should we do now?

t_ch: 我们该回去了。
t_en: We should go back.

t_ch: 等一下！其实虎克还有另外一件宝贝——就在孤儿院那里！虽然虎克也不想卖掉它，但是为了老爹的礼物，这也是没办法的办法……
t_en: Wait a second! I hid another treasure at the orphanage! I don't want to sell it, but if I want to buy daddy a gift, I don't have any other choice...

t_ch: 求求你了，荣誉队员！再帮我找一次吧！就这一次！
t_en: Honorary member, please! Please help me look for it! Just one more time!

t_ch: 拿你没办法…
t_en: Argh, fine...

t_ch: 谁知道还有没有下一次。
t_en: Will this really be the last time...

t_ch: 好耶，我们走吧！
t_en: Yay, let's go!

t_ch: 你自己找，找到了再叫我吧。
t_en: You can look for it yourself and let me know when you find it.

t_ch: 唉，遇到你这么懒的人，虎克也是倒了霉了。
t_en: *sigh* Why are you so lazy? I'm so unlucky!

t_ch: 最多能卖半个冬城盾。
t_en: They're worth half a shield at most.

t_ch: 这也太便宜了吧！虎克立刻放弃了邪恶的念头。
t_en: That's it!? Ugh, I guess I'll have to abandon my evil plan.

t_ch: 没有了，这就是最后一次，我们走吧！
t_en: I promise this is the last time! Let's go!

t_ch: 不准走不准走，要是我们现在就回去了，刚才做过的努力不就白费了吗？虎克保证这就是最后一次了，我们走吧！
t_en: No! Don't leave! If we go back now, everything we've done so far will be for nothing! I promise this is the last time. Let's go!

t_ch: 我现在就想回去了…
t_en: I want to go back now...

t_ch: 这里怎么全是怪物啊？得把他们赶走才行！
t_en: Why are there so many monsters here!? We need to deal with them first!

t_ch: 等等！让虎克看看笔记，我把藏宝贝的地方都标记在上面了。
t_en: Wait! Let me take a look at my notes. I marked the location where I stashed the treasure.

t_ch: 什么东西这么神秘？
t_en: So what is your mysterious treasure?

t_ch: 是老巫婆给虎克的奖励！等我挖出来你就知道了。
t_en: It's a reward from the old witch! You'll know what it is after I dig it up.

t_ch: 「从左边楼梯下来之后，朝着前方走十步，往右转，再走十五步，原地转十圈再往前……」
t_en: "Go down the stairs on the left, then walk ten paces forward. Turn right, walk another fifteen paces. Spin around ten times, then keep walking forward..."

t_ch: 完蛋了…完蛋了！笔记的后半部分全都糊了！
t_en: ...Oh, no... I'm doomed! The second half of my notes are completely ruined!

t_ch: 都怪你写得太多了。
t_en: You shouldn't have written so much.

t_ch: 要是一两句话就能说清楚，那还算什么藏宝图嘛！
t_en: What kind of lame treasure map would it be if it only had one or two steps!?

t_ch: 这就叫多此一举…
t_en: This is useless then...

t_ch: 没有后半部分的笔记，虎克肯定找不到宝贝……
t_en: Without the second half of my notes, how will I ever find the treasure...
*sigh*

t_ch: 其实我也有一件宝贝…
t_en: I have a treasure of my own...

t_ch: 真、真的吗？快点，快拿出来看看呀！
t_en: R—Really? Quick, let me see!

t_ch: （用家用寻物仪帮虎克找找吧。）
t_en: (Let's use the Home-Use Object Finder to help Hook search.)

t_ch: 哇！这个小东西…居然也会说话嘛？
t_en: Wow! This little thing... it can talk?

t_ch: 脾气好差啊，这家伙！不过，如果能帮虎克找到宝贝的话，虎克就原谅你！
t_en: Aren't you sassy! But if you can help me find my treasure, I'll let it slide!

t_ch: 这是什么呀？上面好像还写了字…「给<unbreak>50</unbreak>年后的尤利安」？
t_en: What's this? Looks like something is written on it... "For Julian <unbreak>50</unbreak> years in the future"?

t_ch: 不管啦，既然挖出来了，就打开看看吧！
t_en: Whatever. Since we've already dug it up, let's open it!

t_ch: 这不太合适吧？
t_en: Isn't it unsuitable for people to wear, though?

t_ch: 好像也是哦…那就等<unbreak>50</unbreak>年以后再打开吧！我们先帮尤利安带回去，连着玻璃珠一起交给他。
t_en: I guess you're right... We should wait <unbreak>50</unbreak> years before opening it! Let's take it back to Julian along with the glass marbles.

t_ch: 万一有毒呢？
t_en: What if it has poison inside?

t_ch: 真、真的嘛！老巫婆好像确实说过，有些东西埋久了以后会产生「有毒气体」……
t_en: Really? The old witch did say that some things will emit a poisonous gas after being buried for a long time...

t_ch: 算了算了，还是不打开了！
t_en: Okay forget it, let's not open it!

t_ch: 就是它，就是它！这是老巫婆奖励虎克的大熊玩偶！
t_en: Here it is — my teddy bear! The old witch gave it to me as a gift!

t_ch: 这就是你的宝贝？
t_en: This is your treasure?

t_ch: 没错，它叫「菌菌」！
t_en: Yes, he's called Junjun!

t_ch: 还以为会是什么惊人的东西…
t_en: I was expecting something impressive...

t_ch: 喂！不许看不起「菌菌」！
t_en: Hey! Don't underestimate Junjun!

t_ch: 呜哇，「菌菌」——你怎么这么脏呀，而且还有好几处开线了…不好好清理一下的话，肯定没人会买你的。
t_en: Whoa, Junjun... How did you get covered in dirt? And why do you have so many loose threads? If we don't get you cleaned up, no one will buy you.

t_ch: 这…能卖几个钱？
t_en: How much... can you get for him?

t_ch: 老巫婆说过，「菌菌」可不是一般的玩偶！只要把它打理干净，肯定会很抢手的！
t_en: Well, the old witch said that Junjun isn't just any old teddy bear! He'll be worth a lot of money once he's cleaned up!

t_ch: 没办法了…虎克还没学会缝纫活呢，只有去找老巫婆帮忙了。
t_en: *sigh* There's no other way... I haven't learned to sew yet — I'll have to ask the old witch for help.

t_ch: 真的有大人对玩偶熊感兴趣吗？
t_en: Do adults really like teddy bears?

t_ch: 那是当然！老巫婆说过，「菌菌」可不是一般的玩偶！
t_en: Of course! The old witch said Junjun isn't just any old teddy bear!

t_ch: 唔…虎克还没学会缝纫活呢，看来只有去找老巫婆帮忙了。
t_en: *sigh* I haven't learned to sew yet — I'll have to ask the old witch for help.

t_ch: 要是被她知道虎克又闯进了不该来的地方…呜，又要挨骂了……
t_en: If she finds out I went somewhere I shouldn't have gone... *whimper* Hook will get lectured all over again...

t_ch: 不过就算被老巫婆臭骂一顿，虎克也要把给老爹的礼物准备好！
t_en: But so what if the old witch yells at me, Hook still has to prepare the gift for daddy!

t_ch: 我要对虎克刮目相看了。
t_en: You're starting to impress me.

t_ch: 娜塔莎生气了可别带上我。
t_en: Don't drag me into this when Natasha gets furious.

t_ch: 宝贝找到了，我们快离开这里吧！那些怪物让虎克心里有点发毛，怪可怕的！
t_en: We've found the treasure. Let's split! The monsters here give Hook goosebumps — too scary!

t_ch: 事先说好，你可千万不要把虎克卖掉宝贝的计划告诉老巫婆哦！
t_en: I'm warning you — don't you dare tell the old witch about Hook's plan to sell the treasure!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你先进去，虎克随后跟上！只要你吸引了足够的火力…虎克就能安全突围！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you go first and Hook will follow! As long as you attract enough attention, I'll be safe from the old witch!

t_ch: 放心，我不会让你白白牺牲的！鼹鼠党会记得你的贡献！
t_en: Don't worry, your sacrifice isn't in vain! The Moles will remember your contribution!

t_ch: 哔嘟…「家用寻物仪」查宝为你服务。请使用语音输入目标物件关键词。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Findie the Home-Use Object Finder at your service. Please say the target object you want to search for.

t_ch: 你这个小东西不也在说话吗？
t_en: Wow, you can talk as well, little thing?

t_ch: （眼下似乎也没有更好的选择，看来只能再跑一趟了……）
t_en: (Looks like there is no other choice. Guess I'll have to go again...)

t_ch: （看来史考特想要的是这个盔甲。）
t_en: (Looks like this is the armor that Scott wants.)

t_ch: （尽量速战速决吧。）
t_en: (Better make this quick.)

t_ch: （脏兮兮的盔甲是到手了，现在该回去找史考特要尾款了……）
t_en: (Finally got this dirty armor. It's time to go back and ask Scott for the final payment...)

t_ch: （科鲁泽受了重伤？糟了…有不太好的预感……）
t_en: (Kluzer is seriously injured? Oh no... I have a bad feeling...)

t_ch: （这是…科鲁泽吗？怎么变成这样了？）
t_en: (This is... Kluzer? What happened to him?)

t_ch: （等等，旁边那个是……）
t_en: (Wait, the one next to him is...)

t_ch: 最后…包裹…去……
t_en: In the end... the parcel... went to...

t_ch: 科鲁泽倒在了地上，就像裂界怪物一样消失掉，没能留下痕迹。
t_en: Kluzer fell to the ground and vanished without a trace, just like a Fragmentum Monster.

t_ch: 在他的消失处散落着私人物品，一个未能寄出的包裹上面清晰地写着：「送往磐岩镇，娜塔莎的诊所收」。
t_en: His personal belongings are scattered around where he has just disappeared. "To Natasha's Clinic, Boulder Town" was written clearly on the unsent parcel.
